strengths: fastest Town Center and Dock work rate in the game — insane early eco
War Elephant is one of the highest HP units in the game with trample damage
strong cavalry line with both Paladin and Hussar available
weaknesses: War Elephants are slow and expensive — hard countered by Monks and Halberdiers
no Bracer — archers lose 1 range in Imperial Age
no Hoardings — Castle HP upgrade unavailable
predictable boom opening — aggressive civs can rush before the eco advantage kicks in
unique_unit: War Elephant — massive HP and attack, deals trample damage; slow but nearly unstoppable without hard counters

unique_tech.castle_age: Kamandaran — Cavalry Archers cost gold instead of wood, enabling near-infinite late-game production
unique_tech.imperial_age: Mahouts — War Elephants move 30% faster, partially addressing their biggest weakness

economy_bonuses: TC and Dock work rate increases each age: +10% Dark, +15% Feudal, +20% Castle, +25% Imperial
Start with +50 food and +50 wood — strong opening flexibility
build_order_notes: Lean into the TC bonus immediately — you out-eco almost everyone in Dark and Feudal Age. Fast Castle into Knight flood is standard. War Elephants are a late-game flex pick; avoid over-committing to them if the opponent has Monks or Halberdiers.
common_pairings: Britons — Britons cover the archer gap while Persians bring cavalry and eco pressure
Aztecs — Aztecs handle aggressive military while Persians boom and supply late-game power
Berbers — cheap Berber cavalry synergises with Persian fast TC for a dual cavalry flood

power_spikes.feudal_age: Very strong — faster TC means more villagers earlier than almost any civ
power_spikes.castle_age: Strong — Knight rush backed by superior eco is hard to stop
power_spikes.imperial_age: Variable — War Elephants are game-ending if unopposed, weak if enemy has Monks or Halbs
